title: felicia abyssinica subsp. neghelliensis
intro: Felicia abyssinica subsp. neghelliensis, also known as Neghelli daisy, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to the highlands of Ethiopia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including relieving fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Felicia abyssinica subsp. neghelliensis typically involve making teas, infusions, or decoctions from the leaves and flowers, which are then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
